page: Microsoft Azure M16-8ms is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8280M processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores with hyper-threading (2 threads per core) at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The processor includes a 38 MB L3 cache. The server is equipped with an exceptionally large memory capacity of 438.0 GB RAM, translating to 54.69 GB of memory per core. It also includes 524 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, with no bundled GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates that the M16-8ms prioritizes memory capacity over raw compute speed. Its single-core CPU performance is positioned in the bottom 10% with a stress-ng score of 1037.89 and a Geekbench single-core score of 884.0. Multi-core CPU performance falls in the bottom 25% with a stress-ng score of 5464.92. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only large block speed of 36.54 GB/sec (bottom 25%) and a write large block speed of 31.17 GB/sec (middle 50%). Database operations and Redis SET performance of 5.28 million operations per second both rank in the bottom 25%, reflecting the trade-off of utilizing older or lower-frequency compute resources to support massive memory density.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server is designed for workloads requiring massive memory footprints where CPU performance is not the primary bottleneck. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes it highly resource-dense for memory-bound tasks. It is best suited for large in-memory databases, caching layers, enterprise applications with massive working sets, and memory-intensive data processing workloads that do not require high-performance compute or GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure M16-8ms is a memory-optimized virtual machine designed for memory-intensive workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8280M processor (x86_64 architecture) running at 2.7 GHz, it provides 8 dedicated vCPUs (4 physical cores) and a 38 MB L3 cache. The server features an exceptionally high memory capacity of 438.0 GB RAM (54.69 GB per core) alongside 524 GB of local storage, with no GPU resources. Benchmark results show modest compute capabilities, with single-core and multi-core CPU performance ranking in the bottom 10% and 25% respectively, and memory write bandwidth at an average 31.17 GB/sec. This hardware profile represents a clear trade-off, prioritizing massive memory density over raw processing power. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound applications, making it highly suitable for large in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications with large working sets.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M16-8ms is a memory-optimized server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 438 GB RAM, and 524 GB local storage, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads and large databases.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M16-8ms is a memory-optimized server with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 438 GB RAM, and 524 GB local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine delivering massive RAM density and local storage for memory-intensive enterprise applications and databases.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs (4 physical cores) running at 2.7 GHz
Extremely high memory capacity of 438.0 GB RAM (54.69 GB per core)
524 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Optimized for memory-bound workloads rather than high-compute tasks
Best suited for large in-memory databases, caching, and enterprise apps
